sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: GENERAL ELECTRIC CO는 2026-03-31 분기에 매출 123억 9,200만 달러를 기록했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 매출과 이익 동인은 내부 shop visit volume 증가, workscopes 확대, 스페어 파츠 볼륨 증가, 엔진 인도 증가 및 가격 인상 요인이 함께 작동한 것으로 설명된다.
수익성 측면에서는 장기서비스계약의 추정수익성 불리 조정이 완화돼 2026년 1분기 불리한 변동이 less than $0.1 billion로 보고되어 tariff 충격 완화가 실적 개선에 기여했다.
수요는 상업용 항공운송·화물 수요와 방위비 지원 흐름에 연결되며, 회사는 향후 신제품 플랫폼에서 엔진·서비스 납기 램프업이 추가로 진행될 것이라 설명한다.

riskChanges.text: global material availability와 supplier delivery performance 이슈가 장비·서비스 생산 및 납품을 지속적으로 교란해 수주 전환 속도에 제약을 주는 리스크로 남아 있다.
회사는 공급망 제약과 인플레이션 압박이 계속될 것으로 보고, FLIGHT DECK 및 공급망 협업으로 완화를 추진하고 있어 리스크가 점진적 관리 대상임을 시사한다.
관세는 회사와 공급업체 모두에 추가 비용을 유발한다고 밝히며, 동적 tariff 환경에서 납품 연속성 확보와 가격 조정이 동시에 요구되는 구조로 관세 리스크가 지속된다.

businessChanges.text: 매출·이익이 개선된 설명 프레임은 내부 정비·작업 범위 확대, 스페어 파츠, 엔진 납품, 가격 조정이 동반된 구조로, 단기 수익 원천이 실물 운영활동 확대와 연동되어 있음을 보여준다.
동시에 higher install engine deliveries와 higher growth investment의 영향이 일부 상쇄되어, 성장 신호와 투자 집행이 함께 나타나는 분기였던 점이 중요하다.
RPO는 March 31, 2026 기준 Equipment 9,679, Services 171,376, Total RPO 181,055로 집계돼 서비스 기반 주문잔고 비중이 높은 구조가 확인된다.

financialHighlights.text: 매출은 123억 9,200만 달러, 순이익은 19억 400만 달러로 2026-03-31 분기 손익의 규모가 확인된다.
총자산은 1,284억 4,500만 달러로, 분기 기준 자산규모가 대규모로 유지되고 있다.

watchItems.text: RPO가 Total 181,055인 상황에서 수익으로의 전환 속도는 신규 수주 자체보다 글로벌 공급망/자재 협력의 회복 속도에 더 의존할 수 있어, 다음 분기 납기 지표가 중요하다.
회사는 $1 billion 투자와 5,000명 고용을 계획하고 있어, FLIGHT DECK 기반 대응이 실제 생산성 개선과 비용관리 성과로 이어지는지에 따라 분기별 수익성 기조가 달라질 수 있다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2026-03-31 revenue = 123억 9,200만 달러 (USD 12.392B; 12,392 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 net income = 19억 400만 달러 (USD 1.904B; 1,904 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 total assets = 1,284억 4,500만 달러 (USD 128.445B; 128,445 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31.

Revenue increased due to internal shop visit volume and workscopes, increased spare parts volume, increased install engine deliveries and pricing. Profit increased primarily due to internal shop visit volume and workscopes, increased spare parts volume, price and a lower unfavorable change in estimated profitability on long-term service agreements, primarily driven by the absence of charges. In the first quarter of 2026, we recorded an unfavorable change in estimated profitability on long-term service agreements of less than $0.1 billion, including a $0.1 billion reversal of a majority of the tariff-related charge in the first quarter of 2025. These increases were partially offset by the impact of higher install engine deliveries and higher growth investment. RPO March 31, 2026 December 31, 2025 Equipment $ 9,679 $ 9,773 Services 171,376 154,712 Total RPO $ 181,055 $ 164,485 As of March  Readable Korean unit: 1억 달러.

AAP. Certain of these data are considered “non-GAAP financial measures” under SEC rules. See the Non-GAAP Financial Measures section for the reasons we use these non-GAAP financial measures and the reconciliations to their most directly comparable GAAP financial measures. BUSINESS OVERVIEW AND ENVIRONMENT. As a global aerospace company, our worldwide operations can be affected by industrial, economic, and political factors on both a regional and global level. Demand for our equipment and services is demonstrated by our backlog of engine orders and services and growth in our installed base, and tends to follow commercial air travel and freight demand and government funding for defense budgets. We expect a significant ramp in our delivery of engine units and services for newer product platforms in the years ahead to meet this demand. Refer to the Segment Operations sections for Commercial

Engines & Services (CES) and Defense & Propulsion Technologies (DPT) below for additional detail about these dynamics for our commercial and defense businesses, respectively. Global material availability and supplier delivery performance continue to cause disruptions and have impacted our production and delivery of equipment and services to our customers. We are investing in our manufacturing facilities, overhaul facilities and our supply chain to increase production and strengthen yield in order to improve delivery to our customers. We continue to partner with our suppliers to improve material input, and work with our customers to calibrate future production rates. We are leveraging FLIGHT DECK and partnering with suppliers to improve material input while also proactively managing the impact of inflationary pressure by driving cost productivity and adjusting the pricing of our products

and services. We expect the impact of supply chain constraints and inflation will continue, and we are continuing to take action to mitigate the impacts. However, through FLIGHT DECK and the engagement with our suppliers, aftermarket output and engine deliveries have continued to improve quarter over quarter. We support efforts to revitalize domestic manufacturing and are planning to invest $1 billion in U.S. manufacturing and hire 5,000 U.S workers in 2026, including both engineering and manufacturing roles. At the same time, we support promoting free and fair trade that ensures the continued strength of the U.S aerospace industry. As we operate in a highly dynamic tariff environment, we are focused on continuing to deliver our products and services to our customers. Given our global business, tariffs result in additional cost for us and our suppliers. In 2025, the U.S. established a ze Readable Korean unit: 10억 달러.

generation.costPolicy: Provider chain: Spark OAuth/free first, Gemini free second, approved DeepSeek v4 flash paid last on quota only.
